title: Shared utilities module
description: Create lib/utils.sh with shared helper functions used across all modules. Includes: slugify_title() to convert issue titles to filesystem-safe slugs (lowercase, hyphens, strip special chars), spin() to display a terminal spinner while a background PID runs, and logging helpers (log_info, log_warn, log_error, log_success) for consistent formatted output. File must use set -euo pipefail.
acceptance_criteria: slugify_title 'My [PRD] Issue Title!' outputs 'my-prd-issue-title'
spin $PID displays animated spinner until PID exits
log_info/warn/error/success produce formatted, distinguishable output
File uses set -euo pipefail
All functions are defined (not exported — sourced files share scope)

title: CLI argument parsing
description: Create lib/cli.sh with parse_args() that handles all CLI modes. First positional arg is always the target project directory. Modes: --help (show usage), --issue N (set MODE=issue, ISSUE_NUMBER=N), --discover (set MODE=discover), positional spec name (set MODE=spec, SPEC_NAME=name), bare invocation (set MODE=interactive). Also handles --skip-settings-swap flag. --help displays comprehensive usage including all modes, prerequisites, and configurable environment variables.
acceptance_criteria: parse_args with --help sets MODE=help and show_help displays usage text
parse_args with '/path --issue 5' sets PROJECT_DIR=/path, MODE=issue, ISSUE_NUMBER=5
parse_args with '/path --discover' sets PROJECT_DIR=/path, MODE=discover
parse_args with '/path user-auth' sets PROJECT_DIR=/path, MODE=spec, SPEC_NAME=user-auth
parse_args with '/path' alone sets PROJECT_DIR=/path, MODE=interactive
Missing project dir argument prints error and usage hint to stderr
--skip-settings-swap sets SKIP_SETTINGS_SWAP=1

--help output lists all modes, prerequisites, and env vars (SPEC_GEN_TURNS, SPEC_PASS_THRESHOLD, MAX_SPEC_ITERATIONS, ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW, REVIEW_TURNS, MAX_TASKS, MAX_RUNTIME_MINUTES, MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES)

title: Project validation
description: Create lib/validator.sh with validate_project() that checks the target project has all required prerequisites. Checks: .claude/ directory exists, .claude/CLAUDE.md exists, .claude/settings.json exists, .claude/agents/ directory exists, .claude/skills/prd-to-spec/ directory exists, git remote is configured. Collects ALL errors before reporting (not fail-fast). Error message suggests running configure.sh.
acceptance_criteria: validate_project checks all 6 prerequisites: .claude/ dir, CLAUDE.md, settings.json, agents/, skills/prd-to-spec/, git remote
All errors collected and printed together (not fail on first)
Error output suggests 'Run configure.sh to set up missing Claude files'
Returns non-zero exit code when any check fails
Returns zero when all checks pass
Each error message identifies exactly which prerequisite is missing

title: Entry point orchestrator skeleton
description: Create run-factory.sh as the main entry point. Resolves FACTORY_DIR to its own directory. Sources all modules from lib/. Calls parse_args, then validate_project. Stubs the remaining flow (lock, settings, mode routing, summary) with TODO comments for later phases. Must be executable with bash shebang. Uses set -euo pipefail.
acceptance_criteria: File is executable with #!/usr/bin/env bash shebang
set -euo pipefail at top
FACTORY_DIR resolved to script's own directory (handles symlinks)
Sources all .sh files from lib/ directory
Calls parse_args "$@"
Calls validate_project "$PROJECT_DIR" (after parse_args sets it)
Stub comments mark where lock, settings, config deploy, mode routing, summary will go
run-factory.sh --help works end-to-end
run-factory.sh /nonexistent-path shows validation errors

title: Lock management module
description: Create lib/lock.sh with acquire_lock() and release_lock(). Uses directory-based locking (mkdir is atomic on POSIX). Lock path derived deterministically from PROJECT_DIR so different terminals detect the same lock. Lock directory contains a file with the owning PID. Stale lock detection: if PID in lock file is no longer alive (kill -0), reclaim the lock. Error message for active lock includes the blocking PID.
acceptance_criteria: acquire_lock creates a lock directory derived from PROJECT_DIR path
Lock directory contains a file with current PID
Second acquire_lock on same project fails with error showing blocking PID
Stale lock (dead PID) is automatically reclaimed
release_lock removes the lock directory
Lock path is deterministic from PROJECT_DIR (e.g., /tmp/dark-factory-lock-<hash>)

title: Settings management module
description: Create lib/settings.sh with swap_settings() and restore_settings(). Also create the settings.autonomous.json factory config. swap_settings copies settings.autonomous.json from FACTORY_DIR into target project's .claude/settings.json, backing up the original as settings.json.bak in the same directory. restore_settings reverses the swap. Respects SKIP_SETTINGS_SWAP flag (for child processes in parallel mode). settings.autonomous.json should contain Claude autonomous mode permissions.
acceptance_criteria: swap_settings backs up target .claude/settings.json to .claude/settings.json.bak
swap_settings copies FACTORY_DIR/settings.autonomous.json to target .claude/settings.json
restore_settings copies .claude/settings.json.bak back to .claude/settings.json
SKIP_SETTINGS_SWAP=1 causes both functions to return immediately (no-op)
Backup stored in target project directory (not factory repo)
settings.autonomous.json contains appropriate Claude autonomous permissions

title: Factory-owned config files
description: Create the three factory-owned config files that get deployed to target projects. quality-gate.yml: GitHub Actions workflow that runs quality checks (typecheck, lint, test, build), mutation testing, and security checks on PRs. .stryker.config.json: Stryker mutation testing config for JS/TS projects. .dependency-cruiser.cjs: Dependency architecture rules preventing circular dependencies, enforcing module boundaries, and validating import direction.
acceptance_criteria: quality-gate.yml defines CI workflow triggered on PRs to staging
CI workflow runs quality gate (typecheck, lint, test, build)
CI workflow runs mutation testing via Stryker
CI workflow runs security checks
.stryker.config.json configures Stryker for JS/TS with reasonable defaults
.dependency-cruiser.cjs defines rules for: no circular deps, enforce module boundaries, validate import direction

title: Config deployment module
description: Create lib/config-deployer.sh with deploy_factory_configs(). Copies factory-owned configs to the target project if they don't already exist. quality-gate.yml goes to .github/workflows/ (creates dir if needed). .stryker.config.json and .dependency-cruiser.cjs only deploy when target has package.json (JS/TS project detection). Never overwrites existing files in target.
acceptance_criteria: deploy_factory_configs copies quality-gate.yml to target .github/workflows/ if missing
Creates .github/workflows/ directory if it doesn't exist
.stryker.config.json deployed only when target has package.json AND file is missing
.dependency-cruiser.cjs deployed only when target has package.json AND file is missing
Existing files in target are never overwritten
Logs which configs were deployed and which were skipped

title: Wire infrastructure into entry point
description: Modify run-factory.sh to integrate the infrastructure modules into the main flow. After validate_project: call deploy_factory_configs, acquire_lock, swap_settings. Register a trap handler on EXIT/SIGINT/SIGTERM that calls restore_settings then release_lock. The trap must fire on any exit path (success, set -e failure, signals). Skip settings swap when SKIP_SETTINGS_SWAP is set.
acceptance_criteria: Entry point calls deploy_factory_configs after validation
Entry point calls acquire_lock after config deployment
Entry point calls swap_settings after lock acquisition
Trap registered for EXIT, SIGINT, SIGTERM calling restore_settings then release_lock
Trap fires on normal exit, set -e failure, and interrupt signals
SKIP_SETTINGS_SWAP respected (swap_settings is no-op when set)

title: Spec generation core
description: Create lib/spec-gen.sh with the core spec generation logic (the first half of generate_and_review_spec). Fetches PRD body from GitHub issue via gh issue view. Slugifies title for spec directory name. Checks if specs/features/<slug>/tasks.json already exists and is valid JSON — if so, skips generation. Builds a prompt (written to temp file with quoted heredoc for shell safety) that invokes the prd-to-spec skill. Runs Claude in background with spinner. Validates output: tasks.json exists, is non-empty, is valid JSON (via jq). Returns specific error messages for each validation failure type.
acceptance_criteria: Fetches PRD body from GitHub issue using gh issue view <ISSUE_NUMBER>
Slugifies issue title for directory name using slugify_title
Skips generation if specs/features/<slug>/tasks.json exists and passes jq validation
Prompt written to temp file using quoted heredoc (cat <<'EOF')
Claude invoked in background with spin() spinner
Output validated: tasks.json file exists
Output validated: tasks.json is non-empty
Output validated: tasks.json is valid JSON (jq . succeeds)
Distinct error messages for: missing file, empty file, malformed JSON

title: Spec review loop and retry
description: Extend lib/spec-gen.sh with the spec review loop and failure handling. After successful generation, invoke the spec-reviewer agent. Compare score against SPEC_PASS_THRESHOLD (default 48/60). If below threshold, fix blocking issues and re-review up to MAX_SPEC_ITERATIONS (default 3). If generation fails entirely, retry once with increased SPEC_GEN_TURNS. On final failure, comment on the GitHub issue and add needs-manual-spec label. All thresholds configurable via environment variables.
acceptance_criteria: Spec-reviewer agent invoked on generated specs
Review score compared to SPEC_PASS_THRESHOLD (default 48, from env or default)
Below-threshold specs: fix blocking issues and re-review
Review loop iterates up to MAX_SPEC_ITERATIONS (default 3, from env or default)
Failed generation retries once with SPEC_GEN_TURNS increased
On final failure: gh issue comment with failure details posted
On final failure: gh issue edit --add-label needs-manual-spec
SPEC_GEN_TURNS, SPEC_PASS_THRESHOLD, MAX_SPEC_ITERATIONS read from env with defaults
generate_and_review_spec exported as the module's complete public interface

title: Wire spec generation into entry point
description: Modify run-factory.sh to call generate_and_review_spec in the issue mode route. When MODE=issue, after infrastructure setup, call generate_and_review_spec which sets SPEC_NAME from the generated slug. Then fall through to the execution flow (which will be wired in later phases).
acceptance_criteria: MODE=issue calls generate_and_review_spec before execution
SPEC_NAME set from generated slug after spec generation
Spec generation failure stops the pipeline with non-zero exit
MODE=spec and MODE=interactive skip spec generation (specs already exist)

title: Repository branch management
description: Create lib/repository.sh with setup_staging(), reconcile_staging_with_develop(), and safe_checkout_staging(). setup_staging creates develop from main if develop doesn't exist, then creates staging from develop. reconcile_staging_with_develop attempts fast-forward first, falls back to merge, and aborts cleanly on conflicts (no dirty repo state). safe_checkout_staging handles clean branch switching. Set up branch protection on staging via gh CLI requiring quality, mutation, and security checks.
acceptance_criteria: setup_staging creates develop from main if develop branch doesn't exist
setup_staging creates staging from develop (or main if no develop)
reconcile_staging_with_develop fast-forwards when possible
Falls back to merge when fast-forward fails
Merge conflicts abort cleanly with clear message — no dirty repo state
safe_checkout_staging switches to staging branch cleanly
Branch protection set on staging via gh api requiring CI checks to pass
All git operations use git -C "$PROJECT_DIR"

title: Project scaffolding module
description: Create lib/scaffolding.sh with ensure_scaffolding(). Checks for three files in the target project: claude-progress.json, feature-status.json, init.sh. For each missing file, runs a Claude session to create it. claude-progress.json tracks agent session history and project state. feature-status.json tracks acceptance criteria pass/fail. init.sh installs deps, starts dev server, runs smoke test. Claude runs in background with spinner.
acceptance_criteria: ensure_scaffolding checks for claude-progress.json, feature-status.json, init.sh
Missing files detected and created via Claude session
Claude prompt describes purpose and schema for each file
claude-progress.json schema supports session history and project state tracking
feature-status.json schema supports acceptance criteria with pass/fail status
init.sh created as executable script that installs deps, starts dev server, runs smoke test
Claude runs in background with spin() spinner
Already-existing files are not recreated

title: Task validation and topological sort
description: Create lib/task-validator.sh with validate_tasks() and topological_sort(). validate_tasks reads tasks.json and checks every task has required fields: task_id, title, depends_on, acceptance_criteria. Reports all errors (not fail-fast). Detects circular dependencies (lists which tasks are in cycles). Detects dangling dependency references (depends_on referencing non-existent task_ids). topological_sort returns tasks in valid execution order using a single jq expression with a recursive function.
acceptance_criteria: validate_tasks checks every task for: task_id, title, depends_on, acceptance_criteria
All validation errors reported together (not fail on first)
Circular dependencies detected and error lists which task_ids form the cycle
Dangling dependency references detected (depends_on with non-existent task_id)
topological_sort outputs task_ids in valid execution order
Topological sort implemented as single jq expression with recursive function
Valid tasks.json with no issues returns success
Empty tasks.json or missing file returns error

title: Wire repository setup into entry point
description: Modify run-factory.sh to call repository setup, scaffolding, and task validation before task execution. Flow: setup_staging → reconcile_staging_with_develop → ensure_scaffolding → validate_tasks/topological_sort → commit spec directory to staging. This runs after spec generation (for issue mode) or directly (for spec/interactive modes).
acceptance_criteria: setup_staging called after spec generation (or directly for spec/interactive mode)
reconcile_staging_with_develop called after staging setup
ensure_scaffolding called after branch reconciliation
validate_tasks called on the spec's tasks.json
topological_sort result stored for orchestrator consumption
Spec directory committed to staging (git add + commit) before task execution
Validation failure stops the pipeline

title: Single task execution core
description: Create lib/task-runner.sh with run_task() handling the full lifecycle of a single task. Creates feat/<task-id> branch from staging. Builds a prompt (via temp file with quoted heredoc) that instructs Claude to: orient (read claude-progress.json, git log, run init.sh), implement exactly one task following acceptance criteria and file list, commit with conventional format (feat(scope): description), update progress files. Routes task complexity to model/turns: simple→Haiku/40, standard→default/60, complex→Opus/80. Runs Claude in background with spinner. After Claude: runs auto-fix (pnpm format, pnpm lint --fix) then quality gate (pnpm quality). Returns outcome and failure type.
acceptance_criteria: run_task creates feat/<task-id> branch from staging
Prompt includes: task description, acceptance criteria, file list, test requirements
Prompt instructs orientation: read claude-progress.json, check git log, run init.sh
Prompt instructs: one task per session, conventional commits, update progress files
Prompt written to temp file with quoted heredoc
Task complexity routes to model/turns: simple→Haiku/40, standard→default/60, complex→Opus/80
Claude runs in background with spin() spinner
Auto-fix runs after Claude: pnpm format then pnpm lint --fix (non-fatal errors)
Quality gate (pnpm quality) runs after auto-fix
Returns outcome (success/failure) and failure type (max_turns/quality_gate/agent_error/no_changes)

title: Task retry logic
description: Extend lib/task-runner.sh with retry logic wrapping run_task. Retries failed tasks up to configurable max (default 4, env var). Each retry receives failure context: quality gate output, review findings, exit code, failure type. Provides type-specific guidance: max_turns gets 'continue from where you left off', quality_gate gets exact failure output, agent_error gets error details. Preserves partial work on branch for max_turns failures (no branch reset). Resumed tasks (from interrupted run) detect prior work via claude-progress.json and git log. Time-based circuit breaker checked before each retry.
acceptance_criteria: Failed tasks retry up to MAX_RETRIES (default 4, from env or default)
Retry prompt includes: previous failure context, quality output, exit code
Failure type max_turns: guidance to continue from prior work, branch not reset
Failure type quality_gate: includes exact quality gate output in retry context
Failure type agent_error: includes error details in retry context
Failure type no_changes: guidance to actually implement the task
Retry context written to temp file (not command args)
Resumed tasks detect commits ahead of staging on feat/<task-id> and pass as context
Time-based circuit breaker: checks elapsed time before each retry, aborts if over limit
Returns final outcome after all retries exhausted

title: Code review module
description: Create lib/code-review.sh with review_task(). Runs a separate Claude session using Sonnet model with a completely fresh context (no conversation continuity from implementation). Review prompt focuses on: logic errors, unhandled edge cases, incorrect business logic, weak test assertions, AI-specific anti-patterns. Explicitly excludes formatting, naming, and lint violations. Requests structured verdict: APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, or NEEDS_DISCUSSION. Parses verdict from output. Follow-up reviews (after REQUEST_CHANGES retry) use a stricter prompt that only flags critical issues. Togglable via ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW, turn budget via REVIEW_TURNS.
acceptance_criteria: review_task runs Claude with Sonnet model in fresh session
Review prompt specifies focus: logic errors, edge cases, business logic, test assertions, AI anti-patterns
Review prompt excludes: formatting, naming, lint violations
Review prompt includes full diff (git diff staging...feat/<task-id>)
Verdict parsed from output: APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, or NEEDS_DISCUSSION
Unrecognized verdict defaults to NEEDS_DISCUSSION
Follow-up reviews use stricter prompt (only critical issues)
ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW=false skips review entirely (returns APPROVE)
REVIEW_TURNS configures review session turn budget
Runs in background with spin() spinner

title: PR creation and verdict-based handling
description: Extend lib/code-review.sh with PR creation logic and add code_review as a failure type in lib/task-runner.sh. Creates PR against staging via gh pr create with structured body (task description, acceptance criteria, tests written, review findings). PR body written to temp file and passed via --body-file. APPROVE verdict enables auto-merge (gh pr merge --auto). NEEDS_DISCUSSION skips auto-merge and posts review findings as PR comment. PR creation retries once on failure with 5-second delay. In task-runner.sh, add code_review to the failure type enum so REQUEST_CHANGES feeds review findings into the retry loop.
acceptance_criteria: PR created against staging via gh pr create --base staging
PR body includes: task description, acceptance criteria, tests written, review findings
PR body written to temp file, passed via --body-file flag
APPROVE verdict: gh pr merge --auto --merge enabled on PR
NEEDS_DISCUSSION verdict: no auto-merge, gh pr comment with review findings
PR creation retries once on gh failure after 5-second sleep
code_review failure type added to task-runner retry logic
REQUEST_CHANGES review findings passed as context to retry session
PR number captured and returned for tracking

title: Usage monitoring module
description: Create lib/usage.sh with check_usage_and_wait(). Reads OAuth token from macOS Keychain via security find-generic-password. Calls Anthropic usage API to get current consumption. Implements hourly threshold scaling within the 5-hour API window: 20% at hour 1, 40% at hour 2, 60% at hour 3, 80% at hour 4, 90% at hour 5. At hard cap (default 90%), fully pauses and polls until window resets. At hourly threshold, pauses up to 30 minutes until next window hour. Gracefully skips all checks on any failure (keychain read, API call, JSON parsing).
acceptance_criteria: OAuth token read from macOS Keychain via security find-generic-password
Keychain read failure logs warning and skips all usage checks
Anthropic usage API called to get current consumption percentage
API call failure logs warning and skips
JSON parse failure logs warning and skips
Hourly threshold scaling: 20%/40%/60%/80%/90% across 5-hour window
Usage at hard cap (90%): pauses, polls until window resets, then resumes
Usage at hourly threshold: pauses up to 30 minutes until next window hour
check_usage_and_wait returns 0 on success or skip (never crashes the pipeline)

title: Orchestrator main loop
description: Create lib/orchestrator.sh with execute_tasks(). Iterates tasks in topological order. Before each task: checks if any dependency failed or was skipped (skip with message naming blocking dep). Waits for dependency PRs to merge into staging (polls gh pr view, 45-minute timeout). Detects cancelled auto-merge (PR checks failed) and returns early. Pulls latest staging after each dependency PR merge. Calls check_usage_and_wait before each task attempt. For each task: calls run_task, then review_task (if enabled), then creates PR based on verdict.
acceptance_criteria: execute_tasks iterates tasks in topological order
Tasks with failed/skipped dependencies are skipped with message naming blocking dep
Waits for dependency PRs to merge (polls gh pr view --json state)
PR merge wait timeout: 45 minutes
Cancelled auto-merge detected (PR checks failed) triggers early return
git pull on staging after each dependency PR merge
check_usage_and_wait called before each task attempt
Each task: run_task → review_task → create PR based on verdict
Task outcomes tracked (task_id → ok/failed/skipped)

title: Circuit breakers and orchestrator wiring
description: Extend lib/orchestrator.sh with circuit breakers and modify run-factory.sh to wire the orchestrator into mode routing. Circuit breakers: max task count (default 20), max runtime (default 360 min using $SECONDS), consecutive failures (default 3). All configurable via env vars (MAX_TASKS, MAX_RUNTIME_MINUTES, MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES). Checked before each task. In run-factory.sh, wire mode routing: all execution modes call execute_tasks after repo/task setup.
acceptance_criteria: Max task count circuit breaker (default 20, MAX_TASKS env var)
Max runtime circuit breaker (default 360 min, MAX_RUNTIME_MINUTES env var, uses $SECONDS)
Consecutive failure circuit breaker (default 3, MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES env var)
Circuit breakers checked before each task attempt
Breaker trip logs which breaker fired and stops the loop
Consecutive failure counter resets on success
run-factory.sh routes MODE=issue/spec/interactive to execute_tasks
execute_tasks receives the sorted task list and spec context

title: PRD discovery and sequential execution
description: Create lib/multi-prd.sh with discover_and_process_prds() and sequential_execution(). discover_and_process_prds finds all open issues with [PRD] in title via gh issue list. No PRDs → message and exit. Single PRD → proceed directly (no mode prompt). Multiple PRDs → prompt user for sequential or parallel. sequential_execution processes each PRD one at a time through the standard pipeline flow (spec gen → repo setup → execute).
acceptance_criteria: Discovers open [PRD] issues via gh issue list --search '[PRD] in:title' --state open
No PRDs found: clear message and return
Single PRD: proceeds directly to processing without prompting
Multiple PRDs: prompts user to choose sequential or parallel
sequential_execution processes PRDs one at a time
Each PRD goes through full pipeline: spec gen → repo setup → task execution
Progress reported between PRDs

title: Parallel worktree execution and discover wiring
description: Extend lib/multi-prd.sh with parallel_worktree_execution() and modify run-factory.sh to wire discover mode. Parent process sets up staging branch and deploys quality-gate workflow once before spawning workers. Each worker is a separate run-factory.sh invocation in an isolated git worktree with --skip-settings-swap. Failed worktrees preserved with cleanup instructions. Successful worktrees cleaned up automatically. Parent traps to clean up workers on interrupt. Wire MODE=discover in run-factory.sh to call discover_and_process_prds.
acceptance_criteria: parallel_worktree_execution creates git worktree per PRD
Worktree paths are deterministic (e.g., .worktrees/<issue-slug>)
Parent sets up staging and deploys workflow before spawning workers
Workers invoked as separate run-factory.sh with --skip-settings-swap and worktree path
Workers run as background processes; parent waits for all
Failed worktrees preserved with message showing path and cleanup command
Successful worktrees removed via git worktree remove
Parent trap cleans up worker processes on interrupt
MODE=discover in run-factory.sh calls discover_and_process_prds

title: Logging and temp directory infrastructure
description: Extend lib/utils.sh with structured logging helpers and temp directory management. Create log directory per run: logs/<spec-slug>/<timestamp>/. Provide helpers to write: task JSON log, stderr log, quality gate log, review JSON log, token usage log. Manage status file (task_id=ok|failed|skipped per line) and PR mapping file (task_id=pr_number per line). Create and manage a temp directory (mktemp -d) for prompt files. Temp dir path stored in shared variable for trap cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: Log directory created at logs/<spec-slug>/<timestamp>/ in target project
Helper to write task attempt JSON log: <task-id>-attempt-<n>.json
Helper to write task attempt stderr: <task-id>-attempt-<n>.stderr.log
Helper to write quality output: <task-id>-attempt-<n>.quality.log
Helper to write review output: <task-id>-attempt-<n>.review.json
Helper to write token usage: <task-id>-attempt-<n>.tokens.log
Status file helper: append task_id=status to LOG_DIR/status.log
PR mapping helper: append task_id=pr_number to LOG_DIR/pr-map.log
Temp directory created via mktemp -d, path in FACTORY_TMP_DIR variable
LOG_DIR variable set and accessible to all modules

title: Resume detection
description: Extend lib/orchestrator.sh with resume detection logic. Before executing tasks, check for existing log directories matching the spec name. If found, prompt user to resume or start fresh. For resume: read status.log to identify completed tasks and build a skip set. Detect prior work on feat/<task-id> branches (commits ahead of staging) and pass that context to the task runner. Pass the skip set to the main task loop so completed tasks are skipped.
acceptance_criteria: Prior runs detected by checking logs/<spec-slug>/ for existing directories
User prompted: resume prior run or start fresh
Resume reads status.log to find tasks with status=ok
Completed tasks skipped in the main execution loop
Prior branch work detected: git log staging..feat/<task-id> shows commits
Prior work context passed to run_task for resumed tasks
Fresh start ignores prior state and begins from first task

title: Completion summary and GitHub updates
description: Create lib/completion.sh with print_summary() and GitHub issue update logic. print_summary shows counts of succeeded, failed, and skipped tasks plus the log directory path. When all tasks succeed: close the PRD GitHub issue with a comment listing all PR URLs (read from pr-map.log). When any tasks fail or are skipped: leave the issue open with a detailed comment showing per-task status, PR numbers, and failure reasons.
acceptance_criteria: print_summary outputs: N succeeded, N failed, N skipped + log directory path
Counts derived from status.log
All tasks succeeded: gh issue close with comment listing all PR URLs
PR URLs read from pr-map.log
Any failures/skips: gh issue comment with per-task breakdown (status, PR#, failure reason)
Issue left open when any tasks failed or were skipped
Summary formatting is clear and scannable

title: Post-pipeline cleanup
description: Extend lib/completion.sh with cleanup_completed_feature(). After summary: wait for all PRs to merge (poll gh pr view, reasonable timeout). Delete local and remote feature branches. Remove the consumed spec directory and commit the removal to staging. Clean up the log directory after successful completion (logs are gitignored). Skip cleanup steps gracefully if any fail (don't crash on cleanup).
acceptance_criteria: Waits for all PRs to merge (polls gh pr view --json state, reasonable timeout)
Local feature branches deleted: git branch -d feat/<task-id>
Remote feature branches deleted: git push --delete origin feat/<task-id>
Spec directory (specs/features/<slug>/) removed
Spec removal committed to staging
Log directory cleaned up after fully successful completion
Each cleanup step fails gracefully (log warning, continue to next step)

title: Safety trap handler and final wiring
description: Finalize run-factory.sh with a unified trap handler and complete flow wiring. The trap (EXIT/SIGINT/SIGTERM) must: restore settings, release lock, kill all tracked background Claude processes, and clean up the temp directory. Maintain a PID array (BACKGROUND_PIDS) that modules append to when spawning background processes. Wire print_summary and cleanup_completed_feature at the end of the main flow. Ensure all exit paths (success, failure, interrupt) trigger proper cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: Unified trap handler registered for EXIT, SIGINT, SIGTERM
Trap calls: restore_settings, release_lock, kill background PIDs, remove temp dir
BACKGROUND_PIDS array maintained in utils.sh, modules append to it
Background process cleanup iterates BACKGROUND_PIDS and kills each
Temp directory (FACTORY_TMP_DIR) removed in trap
print_summary called at end of main flow
cleanup_completed_feature called after summary
All exit paths trigger the trap (normal exit, set -e failure, signals)
Cleanup order: summary → cleanup → restore settings → release lock → kill PIDs → remove temp
